status_notes: ipa.go.jp was not blocked in research (2026-07-16) — all documents below fetched successfully. English currency varies by document; see individual notes.
alternate_names: Information-technology Promotion Agency (also styled 'IPA Innovation Platform Agency, Japan' on its current site)
notes: IPA operates several distinct programs registered here as separate match_nodes: SME security guidance, insider-threat guidance, the JC-STAR IoT labeling scheme, the JISEC Common Criteria scheme, and the J-CSIP threat-information-sharing partnership.

match_nodes.description: Information Security Measures Guidelines for Small and Medium-sized Enterprises, and the SECURITY ACTION self-declaration program

data.official_name: Information Security Measures Guidelines for Small and Medium-sized Enterprises
data.notes: Originally published 2009. Current Japanese edition is v4.0 (March 2026); the English translation is v3.1 (older, not yet updated to v4.0). Main guideline body + Appendix 1 ('5 To-dos') + Appendix 2 ('5-Minute Self-Assessment'). SECURITY ACTION (launched April 2017) is a two-tier self-declaration program: One-Star (declare the 5 To-dos) and Two-Star (self-assessment + documented policy). Baseline cyber-hygiene framework for Japan's SME population and a gateway credential for IT-subsidy eligibility.

urls.note: English v3.1 (outdated vs. current Japanese v4.0). Freely accessible.

match_nodes.description: Guidelines for the Prevention of Internal Improprieties in Organizations — insider-threat control design

data.official_name: Guidelines for the Prevention of Internal Improprieties in Organizations
data.notes: Originally published 2013. Current Japanese edition is v5 (April 2022); English translation is only v3.0 (older). v5 covers 33 concrete countermeasures across 10 perspectives (basic policy, asset management, technical controls, workplace environment, post-incident response, etc.), plus case studies, a 33-item checklist, and Q&A appendices. Addresses telework, employment mobility, and Personal Information Protection Act/Unfair Competition Prevention Act amendments. Directly addresses insider-threat control design (access management, data-handling, monitoring).

urls.note: Landing page linking both the current Japanese v5 and the older English v3.0.

match_nodes.description: JC-STAR (Japan Cyber-Security Technical Assessment Requirements) — IoT product security labeling scheme

data.notes: Established per METI's August 2024 policy; officially launched September 30, 2024; STAR-1 applications opened March 25, 2025; STAR-3 requirements released July 2026. Mutual recognition with Singapore's Cybersecurity Labelling Scheme (CLS) began June 1, 2026. Four conformance levels: STAR-1 (baseline, self-declared), STAR-2 (category-specific, self-declared), STAR-3 (third-party evaluated, for procurement/critical use), STAR-4 (highest tier, government/critical infrastructure). Labels carry a 2D barcode linking to product/security info. Harmonized with ETSI EN 303 645 and NIST IR 8425.

match_nodes.description: JISEC (Japan IT Security Evaluation and Certification Scheme) — Common Criteria national scheme

data.official_name: IT Security Evaluation and Certification Scheme
data.notes: IPA acts as the sole Certification Body; NITE (National Institute of Technology and Evaluation) is the Accreditation Body (accredits evaluation facilities per JIS Q 17025); three commercial Evaluation Facilities (ITSC, ECSEC, TUViT) perform evaluations. Based on ISO/IEC 15408 (Common Criteria); certifies software (since 2001), hardware, and Protection Profiles. Japan joined CCRA as a Certificate Authorizing Participant in October 2003. Scheme documents (CCS-01, CCM-01) revised September 2025.

match_nodes.description: J-CSIP (Initiative for Cyber Security Information Sharing Partnership of Japan) — public-private threat-intelligence sharing partnership

data.official_name: Initiative for Cyber Security Information Sharing Partnership of Japan
data.notes: Founded October 25, 2011, in cooperation with METI, centered on critical-infrastructure equipment manufacturers. Per secondary sources (not independently verified via an English primary source): 14 Special Interest Groups (SIGs), 306 participating organizations, plus 3 information-coordination systems; IPA acts as the central information-sharing hub, with NDAs allowing anonymized threat-info sharing. Documentation status: English-limited — only a single brief overview page exists in English (bundled with the related J-CRAT program); detailed annual reports and SIG lists are Japanese-only.

urls.note: The only English-language page found for this program.
